result.stdout:
USAGE:
    ksnitch [PROJECT|SOLUTION] [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    ksnitch Project.csproj
    ksnitch Project.csproj -e Foo -e Bar
    ksnitch Project.csproj --tfm net462
    ksnitch Project.csproj --tfm net462 --strict
    ksnitch Solution.sln
    ksnitch Solution.sln -e Foo -e Bar
    ksnitch Solution.sln --tfm net462
    ksnitch Solution.sln --tfm net462 --strict

ARGUMENTS:
    [PROJECT|SOLUTION]    The project or solution you want to analyze
                                                                     
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                 Prints help information                          
    -t, --tfm <MONIKER>        The target framework moniker to analyze          
    -e, --exclude <PACKAGE>    One or more packages to exclude                  
        --skip <PROJECT>       One or more project references to exclude        
    -s, --strict               Returns exit code 0 only if no conflicts were    
                               found                                            
    -o, --outputsimple         Return results in simple ANSI mode               
    -c, --applycan             Remove the references from 'Can Remove' subset   
    -m, --applymay             Remove the references from 'Might Remove' subset 
    -n, --noBuild              Analyze via solution file without building of all
                               the projects. (Saves the time)                   
    -p, --packageprops         Clear Packages.props from the package references 
                               that are not used in any project                 
                                                                                
COMMANDS:
    version    Prints the Snitch version number
